system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

    location    "constant";

}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.8e-05;
        Pr          0.71;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='alphat', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      alphat;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    innerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (horizontal duct along +x axis)
    // Bottom face (z = -25): 0, 1, 2, 3
    (-50  25  -25)   // 0
    (  0  25  -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25  -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  -25)   // 3

    // Top face (z = +25): 4, 5, 6, 7
    (-50  25   25)   // 4
    (  0  25   25)   // 5
    (  0 -25   25)   // 6
    (-50 -25   25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Corner vertices at x=25
    ( 25  0  -25)   // 8
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 10
    ( 25  0   25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet section (vertical duct along -y axis)
    // Further along -y from the bend exit
    ( 25 -75 -25)   // 12
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 13
    (-500 -125 -25)// 14
    (-500  -75 -25)// 15

    // Top face corresponding to vertices 16-19
    ( 25 -75  25)   // 16
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 17
    (-500 -125  25)// 18
    (-500  -75  25)// 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet horizontal section
    // Bottom face (z=-25): 0->1->2->3 should be CCW when viewed from below
    // That means: 0(left) -> 3(down) -> 2(right) -> 1(up) when viewed from below
    // Top face (z=+25): 4->7->6->5 should follow same right-hand rule
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    // Connects the far end of block 0 (at x=0, y=-25..25) to the bend
    // Using vertices 8(bottom-left), 9(bottom-right), 10(top-right), 11(top-left) at x=25
    // But we need to connect block 0's top face (which uses vertices 4,5,6,7 at x=-50..0) to the bend
    // Actually, block 1 connects the section from x=0 to x=25
    // Block 1 bottom face uses vertices 8,9,10,11 rearranged for proper winding
    hex (9 8 11 10 13 12 15 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: outlet vertical section
    // Goes from y=-75 to y=-125 at x=25..-500
    hex (13 12 15 14 17 16 19 18) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (14 15 19 18)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (8 9 10 11)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
"), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (9 8 11 10 13 12 15 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(9 13 14 10) 4(8 11 15 12) 4(10 14 15 11) 4(9 10 11 8) 4(13 12 15 14) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_A_Amodels/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 62.

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 1 has "inward-pointing faces"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (9 8 11 10 13 12 15 14) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(9 13 14 10) 4(8 11 15 12) 4(10 14 15 11) 4(9 10 11 8) 4(13 12 15 14)
```

This error occurs because the vertex ordering in Block 1 produces faces whose normals point inward (into the block) rather than outward. The issue is specifically with the winding of the hex block.

Let me trace through the vertices for Block 1:
```
Vertex 8: (25, 0, -25)    Vertex 9: (25, -50, -25)
Vertex 10: (25, -50, 25)  Vertex 11: (25, 0, 25)
Vertex 12: (25, -75, -25) Vertex 13: (25, -125, -25)
Vertex 14: (25, -75, 25)  Vertex 15: (25, -125, 25)
```

**Problem:** ALL six vertices of Block 1 lie at x=25. This means Block 1 is a **degenerate block** — it has zero thickness in the x-direction! It's essentially a flat region connecting the horizontal duct to the vertical duct, but with no volume. The "bottom face" and "top face" are both at x=25, making them coincident planes. No hexahedral block can be formed from coplanar vertices.

Similarly, **Block 2** has all vertices at x ∈ {25, -500}, but the bottom face uses vertices 13,12,15,14 (all at x=25) and the top face uses 17,16,19,18 (mixed x-values). This creates a twisted/degenerate block.

### Root Cause

The geometry itself is fundamentally flawed. The three blocks share only edges or single vertices rather than full faces, and Block 1 has zero volume because all its vertices are coplanar at x=25. The squareBend tutorial uses a different vertex layout where blocks share full faces with proper thickness.

### Recommended Solution

The vertex layout needs to be redesigned to create a valid 3D mesh. Here's a suggested approach based on the squareBend tutorial structure:

1. **Add intermediate vertices** to give Block 1 real thickness. Instead of placing all vertices at x=25, introduce vertices at x=0 and x=25 that form proper hexahedra. For example, add vertices at the connection between the horizontal and vertical sections that span the gap properly.

2. **Restructure the entire vertex layout** to follow the squareBend tutorial exactly. A working approach would be:
   - Horizontal duct: from x=-50 to x=0 (width 50 in x-direction)
   - Bend transition: a small region around the corner
   - Vertical duct: from y=-75 downward

   Add vertices that allow each block to have genuine 3D extent. Specifically, Block 1 should connect the top face of Block 0 (at x=0) to the bend entrance (at x≈25), meaning Block 1 spans from x=0 to x=25.

3. **Rewrite the `blockMeshDict` entirely** with a corrected vertex layout. One possible approach matching the squareBend tutorial style:
   - Vertices 0-3: inlet face at x=-50 (rectangle in y-z)
   - Vertices 4-7: at x=0 (exit of horizontal duct, also part of bend floor/ceiling)
   - Vertices 8-11: at x=25 (corner region, spanning y=-50 to y=0 and z=-25/+25)
   - Vertices 12-15: at x=25 but extending further in y (bend section)
   - Vertices 16-19: at x=-500 (far outlet, spanning y=-125 to y=-75)

   Then define hex blocks where each block has vertices spanning a genuine 3D parallelepiped.

4. **Alternatively**, simplify to a 2-block design: one L-shaped block combining the horizontal and partial bend, and one vertical outlet block. But this requires careful vertex positioning.

5. **Keep all boundary patch names** (`inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`) consistent across `blockMeshDict` and all `0/` field files.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading failure

Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh exists. This resolves automatically once `blockMesh` works.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **Redesign the vertex layout** in `blockMeshDict` so that each hex block has vertices forming a genuine 3D parallelepiped (no coplanar-only blocks). Pay special attention to Block 1 — it cannot have all six vertices at x=25.

2. **Ensure boundary faces** in `blockMeshDict` correctly reference the actual faces of the hex blocks.

3. **All `0/` field files** (T, U, p, k, epsilon, nut) must use patch names `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls` — remove `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `calculated` references, and replace with appropriate BC types for these three patches.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
